rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL T JENKS VS. 346 FIRST STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A NON PROFIT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Michael Jenks Motion To Quash Subpoenas
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday July 20, 2018 line 1. Plaintiff Michael Jenks Motion To Quash Subpoenas DENIED. Motion is denied it its entirety as to Lau and Azizuki. Motion is DENIED as to Dr. Milin subject to in-camera review. The documents shall be first produced to Plaintiff's counsel who will review the records and file and serve specific objections within seven (7) days accompanied by a proposed order. Plaintiff's counsel shall then transmit the documents to Department 501. Judge Pro Tem David McDonald shall review the objections and documents in-camera to determine which documents shall be produced and provide a recommendation to judge in Department 501 within 2 weeks. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL T JENKS VS. 346 FIRST STREET OWNERS' ASSOCIATION, A NON PROFIT
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday July 20, 2018 line 1. PLAINTIFF MICHAEL JENKS Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Second Amended Complaint Hearing Required. By no later than 9:00 a.m. on July 20, 2018 Plaintiff to file and serve declaration identifying (and attaching) evidence received in discovery (not previously in Plaintiff's possession) in March and April 2018 giving rise to the new causes of action. Plaintiff shall match the discovery responses with allegations in the proposed amended complaint, e.g. document(s) bates #X and paragraph #Y of the proposed second amended complaint. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: OMAR SHAFIE VS. MEGHAN MCGARRY DANGANAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday July 20, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANT JOBE DANGANAN, MEGHAN MCGARRY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication DENIED. Triable issues of fact exists regarding Meghan McGarry's principal place of residence, i.e. whether it is unit 540, 542, or the entire building. See Defendants' Material Facts 6 ,8 and 9 and all Plaintiff's Material Facts. Defendants' objections to Plaintiff's evidence are overruled. Motion for summary adjudication is denied for an additional reason based on failure to comply with CRC 3.1350(b). = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: JIE LAN TAY VS. ALBERT L. RODRIGUEZ ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Rental; Repair Damages, Attorney'S Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday July 20, 2018 line 3. PLAINTIFF JIE TAY Notice Of Motion And Motion For Rental; Repair Damages, Attorney'S Fees And Costs GRANTED as follows: (1) holdover damages in the amount of $7,075.20 (February 6, 2018 to April 27, 2018) and (2) enforcement of stipulation reasonable attorneys' fees of $1,050 (3.5 hours at $300/hour) and costs of $60. If Plaintiff wishes to recover other damages Plaintiff is to either file an amended complaint for breach of contract/damages or file a separate action as possession is no longer in issue in this action. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ANTON ANCELJ VS. RAYMOND A. SOLOMON ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Deemed Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday July 20, 2018 line 5. PLAINTIFF ANTON ANCELJ Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Deemed Admissions GRANTED, subject to opposition. Sanctions granted in the amount of $600. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: EQUITY GROWTH ASSET MANAGEMENT VS. MELVIN HOLDEN ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday July 20, 2018 line 6. DEFENDANT FABIAN CORTEZ DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for unlawful detainer. Defendant's claim of tenancy (and relevant protections) is an issue of fact not properly raised on a demurrer. = (501/REQ)
